title: Bitcoin vs Ethereum
shortAnswer: Bitcoin leads as digital gold with stronger institutional adoption, while Ethereum drives Web3 innovation through smart contracts and DApps, showing recent outperformance in 2026.

keyDifferences.label: Primary Function
keyDifferences.entityBValue: Programmable network for smart contracts and dApps

keyDifferences.label: Institutional Adoption
keyDifferences.entityBValue: Technology adoption rather than asset allocation

keyDifferences.label: Market Position
keyDifferences.entityBValue: Higher-leverage beta asset with Web3 focus

prosA: Stronger institutional adoption and ETF performance
Established position as digital store of value and macro-hedge
Lower volatility and more stable risk-adjusted profile
Growing Layer 2 ecosystem with Ordinals bringing new developers
consA: No staking capabilities or yield generation
Recent underperformance compared to Ethereum in 2026
Limited programmability and smart contract functionality
prosB: Strong recent performance with 7.12% March 2026 gains
Mature DApp ecosystem driving Web3 adoption
Staking yields of 3-4% annually for holders
More developed and diverse Layer 2 scaling solutions
consB: ETF inflows lagging behind Bitcoin ETFs
Higher volatility as a beta asset
Initial ETFs launched without staking capabilities
verdict: Bitcoin remains the dominant store of value with superior institutional adoption, while Ethereum is gaining momentum through Web3 innovation and recent outperformance. The market appears to be rotating toward Ethereum's higher-growth potential, though Bitcoin maintains its position as the foundational crypto asset.
